summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the speed of electromagnetic waves?

Electromagnetic Waves Maxwell showed that changing E and B fields propagate as waves at speed c = 1/√(μ₀ε₀) ≈ 3 × 10⁸ m/s. Key Concepts — Maxwell’s Equations (Introductory) Maxwell's Four Equations These equations unify electricity and magnetism and predict electromagnetic waves.

What is light, according to Maxwell?

This predicted that light is an electromagnetic wave — later confirmed by Hertz. Key Concepts — Maxwell’s Equations (Introductory) Maxwell's Four Equations These equations unify electricity and magnetism and predict electromagnetic waves. The other options — 'A sound wave', 'A mechanical wave', and 'A gravitational wave' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
